MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Feb-02-2016 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Union Pacific , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-21-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Merlyn Smith and Gloria Smith

RULING:
DEFENDANT UNION PACIFIC RAILROAD COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Union Pacific Railroad Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff's action against defendant is time-barred pursuant to the applicable three-year statute of limitations. (45 U.S.C. 56.) Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff knew, or through the exercise of reasonable diligence should have known, of his asbestos-related illness(es) and their cause(s) more than three years prior to filing this action. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that summary judgment must be granted based on its argument that plaintiff does not currently suffer from any asbestos-related illness. Assuming that defendant's contention is true and that plaintiff currently has no asbestos-related illness, defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff still cannot proceed with his action, as there is no dispute that at a minimum in the past plaintiff suffered from cancer. Even if defendant's argument could preclude plaintiff's action were correct, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not currently suffer from any asbestos-related illness. At a minimum, defendant did not present admissible evidence to demonstrate that plaintiff does not have the asbestos-related pleural disease alleged in his complaint. Assuming defendant had sustained its initial burden, Alvin Schonfeld's November 14, 2014 chest radiograph classification would create a triable issue whether plaintiff currently has an asbestos-related illness. Although defendant titled its motion as a motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication, the court treated it only as a motion for summary judgment, based on the arguments made by defendant, defendant's Separate Statement, and CRC 3.1350. Plaintiff's objections to defendant's Exhibit 1 (medical records) are overruled and his objections to Dr. Moscow's declaration are sustained. If the Court were to consider plaintiffs' evidence, which it does not, defendant's objections to plaintiffs' Exhibits D, E and F would be overruled. Based on this ruling, plaintiff's request made pursuant to C.C.P. 437c(h) is moot. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Tara L. Williams of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Oct-29-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Geoff T. Sloniker of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Merlyn Smith and Gloria Smith
Daniel H. Solomon of DENTONS US LLP (415-267-4000), appearing for defendants / opposing parties CertainTeed Corporation and J.T. Thorpe & Son, Inc.
Erica L. Rosasco of FLESHER McKAGUE LLP (916-358-9042) appearing via CourtCall for defendant / opposing party Union Pacific Railroad Company
Lauren N. Peebles of BERRY & BERRY (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel
Nirali S. Patel of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant Thomas Dee Engineering Company

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(a). The trial date is February 22, 2016 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day of the 120th day is February 26, 2015. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 and the Informal Discovery Conference Order dated July 17, 2015 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is February 11, 2016. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than November 20, 2015. Responses to the demand are due no later than December 4, 2015. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by February 16, 2016 at 3:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:00a.m. on February 18, 2016. Fact discovery cut-off date is February 5, 2016. Expert discovery cut-off date is February 19, 2016. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than November 12, 2015. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than November 12, 2015 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than November 12, 2015 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on January 13, 2016 at 1:30pm. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. (Mini Minutes Continued to next page)
